title: bumelia retusa subsp. typica
intro: Bumelia retusa subsp. typica, also known as the desert bumelia or desert bulletwood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Bumelia retusa subsp. typica include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, wounds, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. Herbal preparations of Bumelia retusa subsp. typica include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the plant's leaves and bark in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
